goal: Build a small local Next.js storefront (Tableau) that sells print-ready digital art posters as instant PDF downloads, targeted at design-savvy apartment renters who want original art at renter pricing ($8–$18). Prove the lifecycle gates can reach a validator-clean seal on this concept and record every point where engineering was required.

target_user.persona: The renter-decorator — 20s–30s, design-literate, apartment renting. Wants real art, not megastore reproductions, but cannot afford gallery originals.
target_user.frustration: Good design is priced at two extremes: unaffordable originals or anonymous mass prints. No middle path exists to buy something craft-made, print it at a pharmacy or home printer, and hang it tonight.
target_user.buying_behaviour: Discovers via browsing, decides on visual appeal + price, instant download, prints locally. Does not need an account for a single purchase.

success_criteria: A buyer can browse the catalog, select a poster and format (A4/A3/Letter), add to cart, complete guest checkout, and receive a download link — all without leaving localhost.
The synthetic cohort (seed=42, 200 users) runs twice with identical results, establishing a frozen baseline.
The QA critique scores ≥ 7/10 with zero P0 findings.
primary_user_journey: 1. Land on homepage — see featured posters and collection browse
2. Click into a poster's detail page — preview thumbnail, artist credit, format options (A4/A3/Letter), price
4. View cart — line item with format, price; empty-cart state if cleared
5. Apply promo code (valid: FIRST15 for 15% off; invalid: shows inline error)
6. Proceed to checkout — email field only (digital delivery), synthetic payment fields
7. Submit order — confirmation page with download button (PDF placeholder); inline error on card decline
8. Access order in session-history — download again if within 30-min cancellation window
9. Cancel within 30-min window (if download not yet accessed) — order cancelled, refund shown as synthetic

design_direction.operator_checkpoint: Checkpoint #2 — operator (Harendra) chose Direction 2 (Tableau) from three options presented on 2026-07-24. Options were: Placard (signal red, grotesque functional), Tableau (sage green, museum-shop), Blot (yellow, studio/zine).
design_direction.signature_device: Artwork-label captions: 'Medium: Digital print · Edition: Open · Format: A3'
design_direction.distinct_from: Archival Warmth (OneReel), Vinyl Pop (Marginalia), Keepsake (Vitrine) — different color temperature, type pairing, and presentation register from all three

A_product_catalog.notes: SKU/id, format variant (A4/A3/Letter), digital, price display USD, inventory state (digital=always available), product detail with artist credit, static cross-sell

B_search_browse.notes: Collection browse, sort, no-results state; NONCLAIM: personalized recommendations

C_cart.notes: Add/remove/format, empty-cart, localStorage persistence, promo entry + invalid-code state, mini-cart aria-live count; NON-GOAL: login cart merge (guest only)

D_checkout.notes: Guest path, email-only (digital), order review + terms acceptance, validation + inline errors, failure states (declined card, network error); NONCLAIM: tax (synthetic/flat); NON-GOAL: shipping address, express wallets

E_payments.notes: PaymentForm proves UI + failure path only; OWNER: real gateway; ENG: idempotency, webhooks, fraud

F_orders_fulfilment.notes: Order state machine: placed→download_ready; confirmation page + download button (PDF placeholder); 30-min cancellation window; NONCLAIM: inventory decrement (digital)

G_returns_refunds.notes: Cancel within 30-min window (if not downloaded); refund state reflected in UI; NONCLAIM: real refund money movement

H_accounts_identity.notes: Guest checkout only; no account registration; NONCLAIM: wishlists (out of scope)

I_trust_legal_compliance.notes: Policy pages built (terms, refund, privacy); OWNER: PCI, GDPR, EU 14-day right of withdrawal; NONCLAIM: content rights (all art is synthetic/fictional)

J_security_fraud.notes: Input validation/XSS/CSRF, no secrets in client; public-safe scan gate (portability wall on Windows — ENVELOPE gap 1); OWNER: HTTPS/HSTS (deployment)

K_accessibility.notes: WCAG AA, contrast check via check-contrast.mjs, accessibility-tree pass, keyboard operable, empty/error states for every route

L_performance.notes: Core Web Vitals discipline; ENG: caching/CDN (deployment)

M_analytics.notes: Event taxonomy view→engage→convert→fulfill→refund; synthetic cohort with hard nonclaim; NONCLAIM: cohort cannot rank UI changes

N_seo.notes: JSON-LD, meta, OG cards, semantic headings; NONCLAIM: real ranking/traffic

O_marketing_email.notes: Transactional email templates designed (not sent); OWNER: real sends; ENG: deliverability (SPF/DKIM)

P_internationalization.notes: USD only, basic date/number formatting; NON-GOAL: multi-currency, regional tax

Q_admin_backoffice.notes: No admin interface in MVP scope

R_content_ux_states.notes: All states: empty, loading, error, success, invalid-id, out-of-stock (digital); trust signals; mobile-first/responsive

S_notifications.notes: In-app confirmations (download ready, order placed); ENG: real email/SMS delivery

T_testing_qa.notes: Golden-path E2E on dev server; edge cases: declined card, invalid promo, expired session

non_goals: Account registration, login, or session management (guest checkout only)
Real email delivery (templates designed, not sent)
owner_gate_stops_identified: Real payment gateway selection and credentials
GDPR/CCPA real consent management and privacy policy publication
Real artist licensing and content rights
engineering_required_stops_identified: Auth security rate-limiting and lockout (not in scope for synthetic local app)
Webhook signature verification and idempotency (no real payment events)

hard_gate_result.primary_user_journey_as_walkable_steps: PASSED — journey written as 9 concrete numbered steps that can be walked on a running dev server
hard_gate_result.design_direction_named: PASSED — Tableau system named with accent, type, signature device
